# ensemble.merge_posterior_tables { #tspaint.ensemble.merge_posterior_tables }

```python
tspaint.ensemble.merge_posterior_tables(tables, samples=None)
```

Average per-haplotype posteriors across an ensemble of paintings.

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**tables**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[list](`list`)\[[dict](`dict`)\[[int](`int`), [list](`list`)\[[Segment](`tspaint.output.Segment`)\]\]\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   One :func:`tspaint.output.posterior_table` per ensemble member; all over the same samples and genome coordinates (sample ids preserved across members).

<code>[**samples**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[iterable](`iterable`)\[[int](`int`)\]]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Restrict to these sample ids (default: those in the first table).

## Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[int](`int`), [list](`list`)\[[MergedSegment](`tspaint.ensemble.MergedSegment`)\]\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Per sample, the ensemble-mean posterior with an uncertainty band, on the common breakpoint refinement. Duck-compatible with ``Segment`` (``left``/``right``/ ``posterior``/``status``), so :mod:`tspaint.validate` metrics score it directly.
